sidebar.deploymentNoId: Deployment started but no id returned

landing.heroSubtitle: Deployher is a self-hosted PaaS that runs on a single VPS node, featuring isolated builds, log streams, and live previews.
landing.signInGithubAria: Sign in to Deployher with GitHub
landing.terminalDemoAria: Animated terminal demo: build, deploy, and logs
landing.highlight2Body: Stream stdout like you are SSH’d in.

why.title: Manifesto
why.p1: All your pet projects don't have to die when the vendor does.
why.p1b3: Vercel's CEO is friends with Netanyahu.
why.p1b4: Heroku saw rapid growth and bailed on free tiers like it was nothing.
why.p2: Every time a platform gets great for us developers:
why.p2b3: Things seem to be enshittified at record speed.
why.p5b: The pet projects you had no where to run.
why.p6a: Some say the easy-deploy era is over.
why.p7a: So I built Deployher. Not to replace the cloud.
why.p7b: But to keep what's yours, yours.
why.ctaLine: So before the roadmap turns on us again,

login.title: Sign in to Deployher
login.subtitle: Continue with GitHub to link your account and deploy from repositories you can access.

oauth.accountAlreadyLinked: That GitHub account is already linked to another Deployher user. Sign out at github.com/logout, or choose another GitHub account when prompted.
oauth.emailMismatch: GitHub’s email on this authorization did not match your Deployher email. If this persists, check GitHub → Settings → Email (primary address and “Keep my email addresses private”).
oauth.unableToLink: We could not link GitHub. Try again from the dashboard.
oauth.sessionExpired: The sign-in session expired. Try signing in again.
oauth.invalidCallback: The OAuth callback was invalid. Try signing in again.
oauth.genericWithCode: Something went wrong ({{code}}). Please try again.

notFound.title: Page not found
notFound.body: The path you requested is not mapped to an app route. Double-check the URL or return to the dashboard.

layoutPrefs.displayHint: Local to this browser. More options on your account page.

dashboard.deploymentUrlsDesc: Host patterns for this workspace. Each project uses its own subdomain or path — open a project for the exact preview link.
dashboard.diagnosticsHint: Process details, memory, and Bun version live on

charts.deploymentsDesc: Success and failed finishes per day · last {{days}} days
charts.previewTrafficDesc: Sampled requests per day · workspace total
charts.noPreviewSamples: No preview samples in this window.

account.heading: Account
account.intro: Profile, linked providers, and how the UI is laid out for you.
account.whereSettingsDesc: User, project, and deployment options are split so each surface stays focused.
account.projectSettingsHint: and use Settings for repo, branch, env vars, and delete.
account.deploymentHint: — open any deployment for logs and preview; append
account.deploymentHint2: to jump to the project-settings hint. Repo-wide options stay on the project.
account.displayCardDesc: Stored in this browser only. Same controls as the header menu.
account.preferredBranchHint: When you import from GitHub, this branch is selected if it exists.
account.defaultNewProjectTabHint: Which tab opens first in the new project dialog.
account.deleteConfirm: Delete your account and all data permanently? This cannot be undone.
account.deleteFailed: Failed to delete account. Please try again.
account.deleteIntro: Permanently delete your account and all associated data.

newProject.heading: New project
newProject.intro: Connect a GitHub repository or add one by URL. We scan manifests on GitHub to suggest framework and preview settings before you deploy.
newProject.noFrameworkPreset: No matching framework preset for this path
newProject.noVercelPreset: No Vercel-style preset match; try another project root or set Framework manually.
newProject.frameworkHintHelp: What we expect to build and serve (Next.js, Node, Python, or static files). Change this if auto-detect picks the wrong stack.
newProject.previewHelp: Static: we serve built files (HTML, assets). Server: we run your app process (SSR, APIs, backends).
newProject.serverPreviewTargetHelp: Isolated runner: previews execute in a separate sandbox container, not inside the app server.
newProject.runtimeImageModeHelp: How we produce the image that runs after build—your Dockerfile, our default image, or auto (prefer yours when present).
newProject.dockerfilePathHelp: Path to your Dockerfile from the repo root (when you use Dockerfile-based deploys).
newProject.dockerTargetHelp: Optional multi-stage target name from your Dockerfile.
newProject.runtimePortHelp: Port your process listens on inside the container (needed for server previews and Dockerfile-first deploys).
newProject.hostBuildHelp: Run our install/build on the worker first, or skip and build only from your Dockerfile (server previews; advanced).
newProject.monorepoDesc: Lockfiles vs app folder (use . for single-package repos).
newProject.manualRepoDesc: Use any GitHub URL you can push to. Branch must exist on the remote.
newProject.linkGithubFailed: GitHub linking failed. Please try again.
newProject.fillRequired: Please fill in name, repository and branch.
newProject.buildPreviewCardDesc: We pre-fill these from the repo scan. Adjust only when the guess does not match how you ship.
newProject.workspaceRootHint: Monorepo: set workspace root for installs and project root for the app. “Dockerfile only” skips the worker build and uses your image as-is (server previews).
newProject.repoCardDescAccess: Choose a repo — we detect the stack from the default branch right away (icons in the list). Pick a deploy branch next; we rescan when it changes.
newProject.repoCardDescNoAccess: Link GitHub with repo scope to list repositories.

validation.pathsInvalid: Enter valid workspace and project paths (repo-relative, no ..), with workspace as a parent of the project.
validation.portInvalid: Enter a runtime port between 1 and 65535 (digits only).

pathExplorer.needRepoBranch: Select a repository and branch to browse folders and locate package.json.
pathExplorer.title: Browse the repository
pathExplorer.subtitle: Open folders until you see package.json, then set workspace (install / lockfiles) and project (the app). Requires a repo and branch.
pathExplorer.treeError: Could not load the repository tree
pathExplorer.usePackageJson: Use folder with package.json as project root
pathExplorer.hint: Workspace must be the repo root or a parent folder of the project. We adjust paths if needed.
pathExplorer.rowWorkspaceAria: Set this folder as workspace root
pathExplorer.rowProjectAria: Set this folder as project root

admin.title: Admin Test Workflow
admin.intro: Run build and deploy for local examples in one click. Open deployment details for logs, or visit preview when ready.
admin.buildSettingsHint: Container limits (memory, CPUs) and per-account concurrent build limit.
admin.accessDenied: You don't have access to this page.

deployment.logQueued: Build queued. Logs will appear when a worker picks up the job.
deployment.startPreviewContainerAria: Start or warm the isolated preview container for this deployment
deployment.runOnlyHint: This page is for this run only.
deployment.runOnlyHint2: cover repo, branch, and environment for new deployments.
deployment.failureSeeBelow: See the build output below for the full log.
deployment.failureSeeContext: See the build output below for context and stack traces.
deployment.runtimeObservabilityHint: for this preview are on Observability.

projectDetail.accordionHint: Values below are read-only on this page. Env vars for deploys live in project settings.
projectDetail.noDeployments: No deployments yet. Click "Deploy" to create one.
projectDetail.setCurrentSuccess: This deployment is now the project current.

projectObservability.intro: Preview traffic is sampled (see sample rate on the traffic card). Client IPs use the {{header}} header only when {{env}} is enabled.
projectObservability.countsByBucket: Counts by {{bucket}} in the selected range
projectObservability.noDeployData: No deployment data in this range.
projectObservability.noPreviewSamples: No preview samples in this range.
projectObservability.webhookAlertsDesc: We POST JSON to your URL when rules fire ({{projectName}}). Same payload shape as the test below.

general.sectionIntro: Manage your project name, repository, and build configuration.
general.workspaceRootHint: Install and lockfile detection run here. Set this to the monorepo workspace root, such as {{apps}} or {{dot}}.
general.projectRootHint: Strategy detection and app build run here. This must stay inside the workspace root, for example {{example}}.
general.runtimeImageHint: Auto prefers your repo Dockerfile when present. Platform always uses a Deployher-generated runtime image.
general.runtimeContainerPortHint: Used for server previews when Deployher runs your built image. This is especially important for Dockerfile-first deploys.
general.hostBuildHint: Dockerfile-only skips host install/build and uploads only the image built from your repo. Server previews only.
general.frameworkHint: Use a framework hint when auto-detect keeps picking the wrong output for this repository.
general.previewHint: This is your requested preview mode. The deployment detail page shows the final resolved output after the build.
general.serverPreviewHint: Server previews are proxied to RUNNER_URL; the preview-runner loads runtime-image.tar from S3 and runs a bounded container.
general.installCommandHint: For Node.js builds only, replaces the auto-detected install command. No shell — use a single command line (examples: {{ex1}}, {{ex2}}). Runs in the workspace root inside the build container.
general.buildCommandHint: When set, runs this instead of the default package-manager build for Node.js. Leave empty to keep using {{pkg}} behavior (including skipping build when there is no build script).
general.repoBrowserIntro: Browse files and line counts for the configured branch and project root.
general.ariaWorkspaceRoot: Workspace root directory inside the repository
general.ariaProjectRoot: Project root directory inside the repository
general.ariaDockerfilePath: Dockerfile path relative to the repository root
general.ariaInstallCommand: Custom dependency install command for Node.js builds
general.ariaBuildCommand: Custom build command for Node.js builds

env.intro: Values are saved with the project and applied on deploy—no per-deploy .env upload. Keys starting with NEXT_PUBLIC_ or PD_PUBLIC_ default to Build; others default to Runtime. Click a scope badge to override.
env.generalLinkAfter: . Saving variables here does not update those fields—open General and click Save Changes after editing them.
env.howBuildP: Build variables are passed into the build step (install, compile). Use for anything the build must read; treat them as sensitive if logs or artifacts could expose them.
env.howRuntimeP: Runtime variables are kept out of the build step and injected only into the running server preview container. Use this scope for server-only secrets like database URLs, API keys, and Next.js server action encryption keys.
env.howOwnersP: Only project owners can read or change these values via the API.
env.parserHint: Parser matches deployment builds (comments, {{export}}, quoted values). Merge from paste or file, then {{save}} when the bar appears.
env.importDropHint: Paste a .env or drag a file here. Matching keys update rows; new keys append.
env.emptyTable: No variables yet. Use {{add}} or import a .env.
env.scopeAriaBuild: Scope: included in build. Click to mark runtime-only.
env.scopeAriaRuntime: Scope: runtime-only. Click to include in build.
env.scopeTitleDefaultBuild: Default: public prefix. Click to force runtime-only.
env.scopeTitleDefaultRuntime: Default: no public prefix. Click to force build.
env.scopeTitleManualBuild: Manually set to build. Click to use runtime-only.
env.scopeTitleManualRuntime: Manually set to runtime. Click to use build.
env.unsavedHint: You have unsaved changes—save before you leave this page.
env.pasteAria: Paste .env content to merge into the table
env.discardConfirm: Discard all unsaved changes to environment variables?
env.fileTooLarge: .env file is too large ({{size}} bytes). Max is {{max}} bytes.

danger.heading: Danger Zone
danger.deleteDesc: Permanently delete {{name}} and all its deployments. This cannot be undone.
danger.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this project? This action cannot be undone.

runtimeLogs.title: Runtime logs
runtimeLogs.runnerNotConfigured: Preview runner is not configured. Runtime logs require a reachable runner ({{runnerVar}}).
runtimeLogs.setCurrentDeployment: Set a current deployment on the project to attach logs to a preview container.
runtimeLogs.notEligible: Runtime logs apply to the current deployment when it is a successful server preview.
runtimeLogs.mainDescriptionBefore: Container stdout/stderr from the isolated runner (deployment
runtimeLogs.mainDescriptionAfter: ). Open the preview if the stream is idle (cold start or TTL).
runtimeLogs.emptyNoSnapshot: No snapshot yet. Turn on Live or click Refresh.
runtimeLogs.snapshotFailed: Could not load runtime log snapshot.

projectDeployments.emptyStatusOff: Select at least one status to show deployments.

repoExplorer.intro: Line counts from a snapshot of the repo (zip download). Binary assets under public/ or static/ are listed but only text files load in the preview pane.
repoExplorer.cannotPreviewBinary: This file is binary or not decodable as text.
repoExplorer.cannotPreviewTooLarge: This file is too large to preview here.

terminal.resetAria: Replay terminal demo from the build tab
